text.title: code-coverage Failure Modes
text.summary: Five coverage antipatterns observed in 2025-2026 CI pipelines.

antipattern.description: Using line coverage as the CI gate metric.
antipattern.reason: Misses uncovered else / except branches. Branch coverage is one flag away and catches the real gaps.
Forces trivial-getter tests, mass mocking, and implementation-mirroring assertions. Coverage % rises; bug-catch rate flatlines.

antipattern.description: Hard repo-total gate that blocks every PR on a codebase with legacy untested code.
antipattern.reason: Engineers learn to skip coverage entirely with `# pragma: no cover` ignores or `--no-cov`. Gate becomes folklore.

antipattern.description: Coverage at 95% on the auth module, but no mutation testing.
antipattern.reason: Tests pass when the implementation is wrong — high coverage, low oracle quality. Mutation testing surfaces this for critical surfaces.

antipattern.description: Coverage produced locally only; no `coverage.xml` artifact in CI.
antipattern.reason: Reviewers cannot enforce the gate; PRs ship with coverage decline noticed only days later, after merge.
